text: 大家好,我是台積電法人關係處的蘇制凱
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 歡迎您參加台積公司2026年第一季的法人說明會
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 由於本法說會是向全球投資人同時連線轉播
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 所以我們會全程使用英文請您見諒
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 好,歡迎您參加第一季的法人關係處的蘇制凱
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 這是Jeff Sue的TSMC Director of Investor Relations
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 您的確保是由於本法說會是向全球投資人同時連線轉播
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 如果您有關於本法說會是向全球投資人同時連線轉播
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 如果您有關於本法說會是向全球投資人同時連線轉播
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 這次的訪問會由於本法說會是向全球投資人同時連線轉播
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 第一季的法人關係處的蘇制凱
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Mr. Wendell Huang will summarize our operations in the first quarter
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: 2026 followed by our guidance for the second quarter
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Afterwards Mr. Huang and TSMC's Chairman and CEO Dr.CC Wei
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: will jointly provide the company's key messages
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: then we will open the line for the Q&A session
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: As usual, I would like to remind everybody that today's discussions may contain forward
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: looking statements that are subject to significant risks and uncertainties
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: which could cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in the forward looking statements
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: So please refer to the Safe Harbor Modestate appears in our press release
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: And now, I would like to turn the call over to TSMC's CFO Mr. Wendell Huang
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: for the summary of operations and the current quarter guidance
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Thank you Jeff, good afternoon everyone
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Thank you for joining us today
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: My presentation will start with financial highlights for the first quarter 2026
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: After that, I will provide the guidance for the second quarter 2026
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: First quarter revenue increased 8.4% sequentially in NT
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: supported by strong demand for leading edge process technologies
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: In US dollar terms, revenue increased 6.4% sequentially to $35.9 billion
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: slightly ahead of our first quarter guidance
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Gross margin increased 3.9% percentage points sequentially to 66.2%
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: primarily due to cost improvement efforts, a high capacity utilization rate, and a more favorable for exchange rate
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Operating margin improved 4.1% percentage points sequentially to 58.1% due to operating leverage
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Overall, our first quarter EPS was 22.0 A and T
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: and ROE was 40.5%
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Now, let's move on to revenue by technology
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: 3.9 meter process technology contributed 25% of wafer revenue in the first quarter
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: While 5.9 meter and 7.9 meter accounted for 36% and 13% respectively
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Advanced technologies defined as 7.9 meter and below accounted for 74% of wafer revenue
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Moving on to revenue contribution by platform
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: HPC increased 20% quarter over quarter to account for 61% of our first quarter revenue
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Smartphone decreased 11% to account for 26%
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: IOT increased 12% to account for 6%
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Automotive decreased 7% and accounted for 4%
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: and DCE increased 28% to account for 1%
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Moving on to the balance sheet
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We ended the first quarter with cash and marketable securities of 3.4 trillion NT
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: or 106 billion US dollars
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: On the liability side, current liabilities increased by 256 billion NT quarter over quarter
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Mainly due to the increase of 129 billion in accrual liabilities and others
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: and the increase of 82 billion in accounts repayable
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: On financial ratios, accounts receivable turnover days was flat at 26 days
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Days of inventory increased 6 days to 80 days reflecting the ramp up of our 2 nanometer technology
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: and strong demand for our 3 nanometer technology
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Regarding cash flow and KPACs during the first quarter, we generated about 699 billion NT in cash from operations
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: spent 351 billion in KPACs and distributed 130 billion for second quarter 2025 cash dividend
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Overall, our cash balance increased 268 billion NT to 3 trillion at the end of the quarter
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: In US dollar terms, our first quarter capital expenditure total 11.1 billion
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: I have finished my financial summary
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Now let's turn to our current quarter guidance
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Based on the current business outlook, we expect our second quarter revenue to be between 39.0 billion and 40.2 billion US dollars
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: which represents a 10% sequential increase or a 32% year-over-year increase at the midpoint
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Based on the exchange rate assumption of 1 US dollar to 31.7 NT, gross margin is expected to be between 65.5% and 67.5%.
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Operating margin between 56.5% and 58.5%.
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Also, in the second quarter, we will need to accrue the tax on the undistributed retained earnings
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: As a result, our second quarter tax rate will be around 20%.
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We continue to expect a full-year tax rate to be between 17 and 18%.
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: This concludes my financial presentation
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Now let me turn to our key messages
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: I will start by talking about our first quarter 2026 and second quarter 2026 profitability
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Compared to fourth quarter or first quarter gross margin increased by 390 basis points sequentially to 66.2%.
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Primarily due to cost improvement efforts, a higher overall capacity utilization rate and a more favorable for-rings change rate.
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Compared to our first quarter guidance, our actual gross margin exceeded the high end of the range provided three months ago by 120 basis points
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Mainly due to a higher than expected overall capacity utilization rate and better cost improvement efforts
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We have just guided our second quarter gross margin to increase by 30 basis points to 66.5% at the midpoint, primarily driven by a higher overall utilization rate and continue cost improvement efforts including productivity gains
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Partially offset by delusion from our overseas fape
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Looking ahead to the second half of the year, given the six factors that determine our profitability, there are a few puts and takes I would like to share
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: As we have said before, the initial ramp up of our two nanometer technology will start to dilute our gross margin in the second half of this year
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: And we expect between 2 and 3% dilution for the full year of 2026
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Furthermore, as the scale of our overseas expansion grows, we continue to forecast the gross margin dilution from the ramp up of overseas fape in the next several years to be 2 to 3% in the early stages and widen to 3 to 4% in the latter stages
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: In addition, given the recent situation in the Middle East, prices for certain chemicals and gases are likely to increase
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Based on our current assessment, there may be impact or our profitability, but it is too early to quantify the impact
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: On the other hand, we will continue to leverage our manufacturing excellence to generate more wafer output and drive greater across-no-capacity optimization in our fape operations to support our profitability
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Also, N3 gross margin is expected to cross over to the corporate average in second half of 2026
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Finally, we have no control over the foreign exchange rate, but that may be another factor
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Next, let me talk about the materials and energy supply update given the recent situation in the Middle East
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: ESMC operates a well-established enterprise risk management system to identify and assess all relevant risks and proactively implement risk mitigation strategies
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: In terms of material supply, ESMC strategy is to continuously develop multi-source supply solutions to build a well-diversified global supplier base and to improve the local supply chain
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: For specialty chemicals and gases, including helium and hydrogen, we source from multiple suppliers in different regions and we have prepared safety stock inventory on hand
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We are also working closely with our suppliers to further strengthen the resiliency and sustainability of our supply chain
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Thus, we do not expect any near-term impact on our operations from material supply
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: In terms of energy, ESMC work closely with tie power and the Taiwan government to ensure a stable and sufficient energy supply
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: With the recent situation in the Middle East, the Taiwan government has announced it has secured sufficient LNG supply through at least May
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: The government has also said it is actively working on securing further LNG supply, diversifying sources into other regions and other power backup plans
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Therefore, we do not expect any near-term disruption or impact to our operations
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Finally, let me talk about our 2026 capital budget
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: At ESMC, a higher level of capital expenditures is always correlated with higher growth opportunities in the following years
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: With our strong technology leadership and differentiation, we are well positioned to capture the multi-year structure demand from the industry mega-trends of 5G, AI and HPC
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We now expect our 2026 capital budget to be towards the high end of our range of between 52 and 56 billion US dollars as we continue to invest heavily to support our customers growth
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Even as we invest for the future growth with this level of KPAC spending in 2026, we remain committed to delivering profitable growth through our shareholders
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We also remain committed to a sustainable and steadily increasing cash dividend per share on both annual and quarterly basis
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Now, let me turn the microphone over to CC
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Thank you, WINDOW
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Good afternoon, everyone
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: First, let me start with our near-term demand outlook
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: We concluded our first quarter with revenue of US-starter 35.9 billion
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Slightly above our guidance in US-starter terms driven by strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Moving into second quarter of 2026, we expect our business to be supported by continued strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Looking ahead, we are very mindful of the impact of rising component prices, especially in consumer and price sensitive and market segment
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In addition, the recent situation in the Middle East also bring further medical, economic uncertainties
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: As such, we are being important in our business planning while focusing on the fundamentals of our business to further strengthen our competitive position
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Having said that, AI-related demand continued to be extremely robust
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: The shift from generative AI and the clear remote to authentic AI and command-in-action mode is leading to another step up in the amount of token being consumed
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: This is driving the need for more and more computation, which supports the robust demand for leading-edge silicon
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Our customers and customers, who are mainly the crowd service providers, continue to provide us with a very strong signal and positive outlook
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: This commission in the March high year AI-maker trend remains high, and we believe the demand for semiconductors will continue to be very fundamental
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Supported by our robust technology differentiation, and broad customer base, we maintain strong confidence for our full year 2026 revenue to now grow by above 30% in US-starter terms
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Next, let me talk about our N2 capacity expansion plan
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Our practice is to prioritize the land in Taiwan to support the first ramp of our newest node due to the need for tight integration with R&D operations
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Today, our new node N2 has already entered high-volume manufacturing in the fourth quarter of 2025 with good yield
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: N2 is ramping successfully in multi-phase phases at both SINZU and Gao Xiong site, supported by strong demand from both smartphone and HPCAI applications
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: With our strategy of continuous enhancement, such as N2P and A16, we expect our N2 family to be a nut, another large and long-lasting node for TSMC
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Now, let me talk about TSMC's global N3 capacity expansion plan
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Historically, we do not add additional capacity to one node once it reaches target capacity
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: However, as a boundary, our first responsibility is to provide our customers with the most advanced technologies in necessary capacity to unleash their innovations
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Based on our assessment, to mean the strong demand in AI application, we are stepping up our KPX investment to increase our N3 capacity
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: We are now executing a global capacity plan to support the robust multi-year pipeline of demand for three-neil meter technologies, which are used by smartphone, HPCAI, including HBN-based-size, automotive and IoT customers
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In Taiwan, we are adding a new three-neil meter to our giga-file cluster in Thailand's size part
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Voting production is scheduled for the first half of 2027
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In our resume, our second file will also utilize three-neil meter technologies
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Construction is already complete, and Voting production will be given the second half of 2027
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In Japan, we now plan to utilize three-neil meter technologies in our second file, involving production is scheduled in 2028
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In addition to all the new files, we continue to convert five nanometer tools to support three nanometer capacity in Taiwan
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: We are also leveraging our manufacturing excellence to drive greater productivity across our file in all locations to generate more wafer output
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: We are also focusing on capacity optimization across nodes, which include inflexible capacity support among the N7 and 5 and N3 nodes
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: We are using multiple levers to do everything we can, wherever we can, however we can to maximize the support to all our customers across all platforms
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Also, let me emphasize that, while the capacity is tight, we do not pick and choose or play favories among our customers
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Next, let me talk about our mature node strategies
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: SMC's strategies and mature node has not changed
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Our focus is to build a high-eal capacity for specialized technologies rather than just normal capacity
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: For example, we are increasing our mature node capacity, such as in JASM51 in Japan for similar sensor applications and ESSMC in Germany for automotive and industrial applications
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Meanwhile, we have planned to wind down our FET2, which is 16-FET and FET5, which is 8-FET, focused on gotten nitrate and used a valuable space to optimize the support for leading-edge applications
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Even without FET2 and FET5, we still have enough capacity to fully support our existing customers
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In summary, our strategy will be to continue to optimize our capacity, capacity means, which in mature nodes, and focusing on the higher value added in strategic segment
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In short, we have a necessary capacity to support our customers' growth
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Finally, let me talk about our A14 status
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Featuring our second generation nanosheet, so-and-sister structure, A14 was delivered another four node stride from N2, with performance and power benefit to a cross
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: To address the essential need for high performance and energy efficient computing
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: In P10 and P10, A14 will provide 10-15 speed improvement at the same power, or 25-30 power improvement at the same speed, and close to 20% shift density gain
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Our A14 technology development is on track and progress in oil. We are observing a high level of customer interest and engagement from both smartphone and HPC applications
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Voting production is scheduled for 2021-28
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Our A14 technology and its derivative will further extend our technology leadership position
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: And enable TSMC to capture the growth opportunities were into the future
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: This concludes our key message, and thank you for your attention
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Thank you, CC. This concludes our prepared statements
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Before we begin the question and answer session, I would like to remind everybody to please limit your questions to two at a time to allow all the participants an opportunity to ask their questions
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Should you wish to raise your question in Chinese, I will translate it to English before our management answers your question
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: For those of you on the call, if you would like to ask a question, please press the star then one on your telephone keypad now
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: If at any time you'd like to remove yourself from the questioning queue, please press star two
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Now let's begin the Q&A session operator, can we proceed with the first participant on the line, please, thank you
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: The first one to ask questions has been from Vancouver, America
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Good afternoon, CC, Wendell, and Jeb, congrats on the solid results and guidance, and thanks for taking my questions
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: I would like to start with your three-millimeter growth margins, I'll look
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: You just mentioned the node is going to across the appropriate average growth margin in second half this year
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: We should now add 60% to levels, and we'll understand the technology using severe under supply and back by strong AID man
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: And you already forecasted the capacity expansion through conversion and greenfield through 1028
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Would you be able to discuss more in detail what kind of applications are driving such strong business for you and convince you to extend more?
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: And the other thing, three-millimeter as well, it's just the node started to run from Fort Corridor 222
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Which means some of your equipment will be fully depreciated by 2027
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Should we expect the node margins to be trending even higher with very solid use, I think, and also pricing trend?
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Thank you
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Okay, so the first question from Haas, Liu, a think of America, it's two parts on three nanometer
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: First, SCC described we are executing a plan for expanding three nanometer capacity
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: So he wants to understand what are the applications to drive such a strong multi-year looking ahead
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Pipeline of demand for three nanometer since it's already been around in volume production since late 22
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: That's the first part of his question
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Who are the answers that? I think the application is simple, it's still the HPC AI applications
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Is that answer your question?
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Okay, yes, that is the first part, and that's the second part of his question is on the gross margin for three nanometer
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: His question is really, what is the gross margin outlook for three nanometer?
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Will it cross over in the second half of this year to what level, and then once it becomes fully depreciated, what happens to the margin?
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Okay, this is random
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: We expect the M3 gross margin to reach and cross the corporate gross margin level in the second half of this year
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: And we don't have a number to share with you, but after the fully depreciation as our previous note, the gross margin are generally very high
speaker: WENDELL HUANG - CFO

text: Okay, that's, I'll take that as 1.5 questions, so if you have a quick follow-up for your second question
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Yes, yes, thanks so much, and the other, I think just a 0.5 follow-up is probably just the key packs you revise up to the high end of your guidance for 52 to 56 billion years others for this year
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Compared to 3 months ago, what gives you the incremental confidence when you discuss with your customers and also customers' customers regarding the demand outlook to support your stronger or the upper half of your guidance for the CapEX this year?
speaker: HAAS LIU - ANALYST, BANK OF AMERICA

text: Okay, thank you, Ha, so his second question is he notes that indeed we have this time guided to the high end of our CapEX range versus January
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: So what incrementally is driving this revision to the CapEX what gives us the confidence to go to the high end of the 52 to 56 billion range?
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Thank you
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Well, again, DCCC where the me answer this question, a simple, a very simple answer is
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: The demand are very robust, especially from the SPC and AI applications and also we try very hard to speed it up and pulling all the equipment as we can
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Still our supplies are very tight, the demand is continue to increase and so we continue to work with our suppliers to speed it up
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: And that's why we are toward our high end of CapEX forecast
speaker: DR. C.C. WEI - CHAIRMAN & CEO

text: Okay, Ha, does that answer your question?
speaker: JEFF SU - IR DIRECTOR

text: Yes, thank you so much, DCCC, I will be back in the day

text: Sure, thank you, operator can we move on to the next participant please

text: Next one, the last question, Goku, Hari, Hari, JPMorgan
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Hi, I'm Professor known, I'm the event manager, my first question on your comments on demand, clearly demand is even better than what you predicted back in January, DC
speaker: GOKUL HARIHARAN - ANALYST, JPMORGAN

text: And you also raised the CapEX, now all your customers seem to be telling everybody they can tell that they first to remain the biggest constraint
speaker: GOKUL HARIHARAN - ANALYST, JPMORGAN

text: So given your expanded 300 meter to past deep plan and faster CapEX, DC what is your expectation that how long does the flight constraint is likely to last?
speaker: GOKUL HARIHARAN - ANALYST, JPMORGAN
